name: Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco
region_intro: Jogging routes around Nuñomoral traverse a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, natural parks, and scenic viewpoints. The region, part of the Sierra de Francia Natural Park, offers a mix of moderate trails and more challenging ascents. Runners can explore paths alongside waterways like the Río de los Ángeles and Alagón River, or venture towards elevated viewpoints. This varied terrain provides opportunities for different running experiences, from riverside paths to routes with significant elevation gain. Best jogging routes around Nuñomoral The most popular jogging route is Chorro de las Batuecas via the Valle de las Batuecas Route - Batuecas and Sierra de Francia Natural Park, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views within the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco, a moderate 1.6 miles (2.6 km) path. This shorter route leads to the Chorro de la Meancera waterfall. Local runners also love the Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga, a 7.9 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Nuñomoral is defined by river trails, natural park landscapes, and routes leading to panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails with considerable climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Nuñomoral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Nuñomoral?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 curated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Nuñomoral in this guide, offering a variety of experiences from riverside paths to trails with significant elevation gain. These routes are primarily moderate to difficult, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Nuñomoral?
A: While there are no routes explicitly graded as 'easy' in this guide, several moderate options can be suitable for beginners looking for a gentle introduction to running in the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991203199/chorro-de-la-meancera-desde-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate path leading to a waterfall, which might be a good starting point.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Nuñomoral?
A: The running trails around Nuñomoral traverse a diverse landscape. You'll find paths alongside waterways like the Río de los Ángeles and Alagón River, as well as routes venturing into the Sierra de Francia Natural Park with more challenging ascents and panoramic viewpoints. The terrain varies from river valleys to more elevated, rugged sections.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, Nuñomoral's routes offer stunning natural features. You can run to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4511250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera&lt;/a&gt; waterfall via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991203199/chorro-de-la-meancera-desde-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; route. For panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990941014/mirador-del-pico-blanco-circular-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4386882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Blanco Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running routes in Nuñomoral suitable for families?
A: For families, shorter moderate routes are generally recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991203199/chorro-de-la-meancera-desde-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively short (1.6 miles) and moderate option that leads to a waterfall, which can be an engaging destination for children. Always consider the fitness level of all family members before choosing a route.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails around Nuñomoral?
A: Many trails in natural areas like the Sierra de Francia Natural Park are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Nuñomoral area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990941014/mirador-del-pico-blanco-circular-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 7.9-mile trail with significant elevation gain, offering varied terrain and views.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners seeking a challenge can explore routes with considerable climbs and longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990941014/mirador-del-pico-blanco-circular-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Pico Blanco – circular from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.9-mile route with significant elevation. Another longer option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991227952/rio-de-los-angeles-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río de los Ángeles River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.2-mile path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Nuñomoral?
A: The best times for running in Nuñomoral are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, so if running during this season, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the midday heat. Winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Nuñomoral?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as trailheads for these routes, such as El Gasco or La Pesga. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially when visiting natural parks.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow a river or offer waterside views?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by its river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991227952/rio-de-los-angeles-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río de los Ángeles River Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive running alongside the river. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Meandro del Melero – Alagón River loop from Piscina Natural de Riomalo&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views of the Alagón River.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Nuñomoral?
A: Over 40 runners have used komoot to explore Nuñomoral's varied terrain. They often praise the diverse landscape, from tranquil river paths to challenging ascents with rewarding views within the Sierra de Francia Natural Park. The routes are appreciated for showcasing the best of the region's natural beauty.
tips:
The Chorro de la Meancera is an impressive waterfall located in the hamlet of El Gasco, in the region of Las Hurdes, Cáceres. This waterfall is one of the highest and most aesthetic in the region, with a drop of approximately 100 meters.
The best time to visit the Chorro de la Meancera is from autumn to spring, when the water flow is greater due to the rains. In summer, the flow may be lower, but the beauty of the place is still impressive.
